title: ophrys apifera f. curviflora
intro: Ophrys apifera f. curviflora, also known as the bent bee orchid, is a rare and unique subspecies of the bee orchid plant. It is often found in damp meadows and is known for its distinctive curved flowers that resemble a bee. The medicinal properties of the bent bee orchid include its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and skin issues. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal preparations such as infusions and salves, which are believed to provide health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and enhanced immune function.
